description: Ward approaches Laura Hayward at her university campus under the pretense of being a lottery agent. Laura is immediately suspicious but makes a call to her brother as soon as Ward walks away, providing the team with a location ping for Centipede’s new base in Oakland, California. The team successfully tracks this lead remotely by eavesdropping on her call.

transcript.line: Laura Hayward?
That's me.
Sorry to bother you, But I was hoping you could help me out.
I hope so, too.
I'm looking for you brother, Brian. Have you seen him lately?
I'm ... I'm sorry. Who are you?
Ah. Dan Filch, Ohio State gaming commission. We're trying to contact Brian, tell him the good news.
He won the lottery?
Really?
Mm-hmm.
And you're looking for him? Don't you guys want to keep the money?
Sure, but we have to at least try and contact the winners before the ticket expires. Your brother's does in six days, so if you know where he is...
I don't. Sorry. We're not close. I haven't spoken to him since he got back from Afghanistan.
Well, now you have a reason to... 50,000 reasons, actually. So let us know if you hear from him.
Okay.
Not sure why you wanted to go with the lottery story, sir. It's a tough sell.
Exactly. Nothing makes people more suspicious than a handsome man offering them free money. She's making the call now.
Brian, it's me. Some guy was just waiting for me at school, said you won the lottery. I know, right? We're not that lucky.
Is he local?
No. He's in Oakland, California.
Come in!
